differential_diagnoses: Infectious (mycotic) aortic aneurysm with threatened rupture
Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia with associated vertebral osteomyelitis/epidural abscess
Parasternal septic arthritis or sternoclavicular osteomyelitis
next_steps: Urgent consultation with Vascular Surgery and Infectious Disease
Repeat CT angiography of the chest/abdomen to assess for rapid progression of the aortic mural thickening/pseudoaneurysm
Transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
MRI of the thoracic spine with contrast to rule out spondylodiscitis and epidural abscess
Continued serial blood cultures to document clearance of S. aureus bacteremia
Hemodynamic optimization and blood pressure control, monitoring for signs of contained or free aortic rupture
